pattern: Count one bits per number and test that count for primality; since values stay small, the set-bit count is tiny and a fixed set of small primes covers every case.
hints: For each number in the range you only need how many 1 bits it has, a value that never grows large here.
Because the maximum value bounds the bit width, the possible set-bit counts are small, so primality reduces to membership in a short list of small primes.
Iterate the range, pop-count each number, and add one whenever that count is a prime like 2, 3, 5, 7, 11, 13, 17, or 19.

edge_inputs.description: small range from the first example
overlapping range from the second example
single value with one set bit, not prime
single value with two set bits, prime
powers of two have one set bit and never count
wide range near the lower bound
range touching the upper value bound

stress.description: random valid ranges with bounded width
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    left = rng.randint(1, 1000000)
    width = rng.randint(0, min(10000, 1000000 - left))
    right = left + width
    return [left, right]
stress.note: respects the right-minus-left width bound and the value range
